mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate revenue beats and pushing S&P 500 prices higher over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls confirms a broad-based economic expansion. This dual confirmation reduces recession risk premiums and sustains equity risk appetite, lifting S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when both proxies rise concurrently, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, industrial production growth decelerates as capacity constraints bind. This supply-side slowdown compresses corporate margins and triggers mean-reversion selling in cyclical equities, depressing S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO deceleration consistently coincides with positive S&P 500 5-day returns, or the signal's information coefficient remains below 0.02 across multiple late-cycle episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent annualized signals robust aggregate demand. This growth trajectory supports earnings revisions upward, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 2.5%, or the signal exhibits no predictive power after controlling for market beta.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll additions directly reflect current economic momentum. Strong employment data reduces uncertainty and attracts short-term institutional buying, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The sign of PAYEMS changes consistently contradicts the sign of 5-day SPX returns, or the rank correlation between payroll surprises and forward returns remains below 0.05 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with accelerating CPI inflation signal an aggressive monetary tightening cycle. This combination compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and lower forward earnings multiples, driving negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint increase in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 60-day equity returns, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in residential construction financing indicates tightening credit conditions for housing developers. This credit contraction precedes broader economic slowdowns, depressing equity valuations and yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines by more than 5% over a 3-month window
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances following a 5% RSAFS drop, or the signal's hit rate does not exceed 50% over a 10-year backtest.
